---
title: Cloudflare KV
description: Cache backed by a Cloudflare KV namespace for Workers.
icon: Globe
---

## When to use

Use the Cloudflare KV adapter inside a Cloudflare Worker. KV is a globally distributed key-value store with eventual consistency.

## Installation

```bash
bun add @justwant/cache
```

No additional packages required — the KV namespace is injected from the Worker environment.

## Setup

Bind a KV namespace in `wrangler.toml`:

```toml title="wrangler.toml"
[[kv_namespaces]]
binding = "CACHE"
id = "YOUR_KV_NAMESPACE_ID"
```

Create the cache inside your Worker using the bound namespace:

```ts title="src/index.ts"
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { cfKvAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/cf-kv";

export default {
  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
    const cache = createCache({
      adapter: cfKvAdapter({ kv: env.CACHE }),
    });

    const value = await cache.wrap("key", () => fetchData(), { ttl: "5m" });
    return Response.json(value);
  },
};
```

## Options

```ts
cfKvAdapter({
  kv: CloudflareKVNamespace;  // Cloudflare KV namespace binding (required)
  keyPrefix?: string;         // Key prefix applied to all entries
})
```

## Notes

- Cloudflare KV has **eventual consistency** — writes may take up to 60 seconds to propagate globally.
- TTL is enforced via KV's native expiration (`expirationTtl` for relative TTL, `expiration` for absolute `Date`).
- Tag invalidation is **not supported** — `invalidateTag` is a no-op on this adapter.
